mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show a positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, which erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing activity and supply-side recovery, which generates positive short-term momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation crosses above 0.6%, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in the majority of months where CPIAUCSL MoM change surpasses 0.6%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns, or the interaction effect is negative when tested against 60-day forward returns in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, which acts as a leading indicator of economic deceleration and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or the predictive relationship breaks down during periods of housing supply shortages.
